data.description:
Profit before tax rose 32% to £31.8m, driven by strong recoveries in both motor finance and property lending. Impairment charges fell sharply, net assets hit a record £249m, and a final dividend of 45p per share is proposed.

data.title: ZAWYA: Abu Dhabi-listed Investcorp Capital invests $200mln in US residential real estate
data.description:
Staff Writer

Investcorp Capital (ICAP), the Abu Dhabi-listed alternative investment firm, has acquired a $200 million investment in US residential real estate to capitalise on the growing demand for senior living and multifamily housing, it said.

The portfolio acquisition comprises a multifamily community in New Jersey, and two senior housing properties in Orange County, California and Long Island, New York.

ICAP has also confirmed it is targeting “significant” additional investment in senior housing over the next three years, with assets poised to capitalise on “favourable demographics and limited new supply.”

In December, ICAP acquired a diversified industrial portfolio with properties located across coastal markets in both the eastern and western US for approximately $400 million. At the time, Yusef Al Yusef, Global Head of Distribution at ICAP said that the group aims to invest no less than $6 billion during its current fiscal year, spanning from 1 July 2025 to 30 June 2026.

(Writing by Bindu Rai, editing by Seban Scaria)

bindu.rai@lseg.com

Disclaimer: This article is provided for informational purposes only. The content does not provide tax, legal or investment advice or opinion regarding the suitability, value or profitability of any particular security, portfolio or investment strategy. Read our full disclaimer policy here.

data.title: Malaysia KLCI Could Hit 1840 Though Path May Be Choppy — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's benchmark KLCI index could touch 1840 by year-end, but the path could be choppy until the Middle East conflict de-escalates, Citi analyst Megat Fias says in a note. The bank sees Malaysia to be in a relatively better position compared with its peers given it is a net energy exporter, but that could be offset by potential fiscal pressure because of the government's subsidies. Citi likes consumer and construction sector stocks due to their good earnings visibility, while healthcare and telecom companies provide defensiveness. The plantation sector is rated overweight as a hedge to rising oil prices. The KLCI is up 0.6% at 1712.28. (venkat.pr@wsj.com)

data.title: OCI Global Sells Remaining Shares of Methanex Corporation
data.description:
AMSTERDAM, April 21, 2026 /PRNewswire/ — OCI Global , ("OCI") announced today that its wholly owned subsidiary OCI Chemicals B.V. has sold its remaining 2,612,962 common shares ("Shares") of Methanex Corporation ("Methanex"), representing approximately 3.4% of the issued and outstanding Shares (the "Disposition"). The sale price was US$52.85 per Share, resulting in net proceeds of approximately US$138.1 million, after customary fees and expenses. The Disposition was made on 20 April as a block trade.

As a result of the Disposition, OCI no longer owns or exercises control or direction over any Methanex Shares.

About OCI Global

Learn more about OCI at www.oci-global.com. You can also follow OCI on LinkedIn.

Additional Information

OCI effected the Disposition for investment purposes. Depending on market conditions and other factors, including Methanex's business and financial condition, OCI may in future acquire securities of Methanex or sell some or all of the securities of Methanex that it may own at any such time.

Cautionary Statement on Forward-Looking Information

Certain information contained in this press release, including any information relating to OCI's investment in Methanex, constitutes "forward-looking statements". All statements, other than statements of historical fact, are forward-looking statements. The words "may" and "will" and similar expressions identify forward-looking statements. In particular, this press release contains forward-looking statements including, without limitation, with respect to the potential for OCI to acquire securities of Methanex in future or to dispose of some or all of such securities that OCI owns at any time. Forward-looking statements are necessarily based upon a number of assumptions, including material assumptions considered reasonable by OCI as at the date of this press release in light of management's experience and perception of current conditions and expected developments, and are inherently subject to significant business, economic, and competitive uncertainties and contingencies.

Many of these uncertainties and contingencies can affect our actual results and could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied in any forward-looking statements made by, or on behalf of, us. Readers are cautioned not to put undue reliance on forward-looking statements which are not guarantees of future events, and speak only as of the date made. All of the forward-looking statements made in this press release are qualified by these cautionary statements. OCI disclaims any intention or obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise, except as required by applicable law.

View original content:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/oci-global-sells-remaining-shares-of-methanex-corporation-302748281.html

SOURCE OCI Global

data.title: Elisa Oyj Bottom Line Advances In Q1
data.description:
HELSINKI (dpa-AFX) - Elisa Oyj (EIA.F) revealed a profit for its first quarter that Increased, from last year

The company's earnings came in at EUR91.4 million, or EUR0.57 per share. This compares with EUR90 million, or EUR0.56 per share, last year.

The company's revenue for the period fell 1.3% to EUR548.4 million from EUR555.8 million last year.

Elisa Oyj earnings at a glance (GAAP) :

-Earnings: EUR91.4 Mln. vs. EUR90 Mln. last year.

-EPS: EUR0.57 vs. EUR0.56 last year.

-Revenue: EUR548.4 Mln vs. EUR555.8 Mln last year.

This decline in first-quarter revenue reflects lower equipment sales, due to challenging economic environment and increased device prices as a result of higher component costs.

Looking ahead, for the full year, the company has reaffirmed its guidance.

Elisa Oyj, said: 'Full-year revenue is estimated to be at the same level as or slightly higher than in 2025. Full-year comparable EBITDA is anticipated to be EUR 815-845 million. Capital expenditure is expected to be 12 per cent of revenue.'

data.title: U.K. Unemployment Falls Below 5%
data.description:
By Don Nico Forbes

The U.K.'s unemployment rate fell in the three months through February, a show of surprising resilience as the conflict in the Middle East raises the pressure on firms to reduce hiring or cut jobs.

The unemployment rate was 4.9%, compared with 5.2% in the three months through January, the Office for National Statistics said Tuesday. The rate was last lower in the three months to August 2025.

Annual wage growth, excluding bonuses, was 3.6%, compared with 3.5% in the previous period.

A consensus of economists polled by The Wall Street Journal expected a jobless rate of 5.2% and wage growth at 3.5%.

Write to Don Nico Forbes at don.forbes@wsj.com

data.title: Beiersdorf sales slip as Nivea and La Prairie struggle in first quarter
data.description:
German consumer goods group Beiersdorf reported a drop in its quarterly sales on Tuesday, hurt by soft growth in its flagship Nivea brand and disruptions in Chinese travel retail and U.S. department stores for its luxury La Prairie brand.

Sales of Nivea, the company's flagship skin and body care brand, fell 7% organically due to trade-related tensions in Europe and a challenging mass market, while sales of its Eucerin and Aquaphor brands jumped 8.2% organically thanks to recent product innovations, Beiersdorf said.

It reported a 14.9% organic drop in sales of La Prairie after disruptions in the U.S. department store channel and in travel retail in China, both factors that the Hamburg-based company said were temporary.

The group's first-quarter revenue fell 4.6% organically to 2.48 billion euros ($2.92 billion), compared with 2.69 billion euros a year earlier.

The skincare company confirmed its guidance for 2026 and said positive dynamics in sales from retailers to the final consumer indicated an improvement over the coming quarters after underperformance in the first three months of the year.

($1 = 0.8489 euros)

data.title: UK's Associated British Foods H1 adj. EPS misses on retail margin pressure
data.description:
Overview

  • UK food and retail group's H1 revenue slightly missed analyst expectations, declining 2% yr/yr

  • Adjusted EPS and adjusted operating profit for H1 missed analyst expectations

  • Company to demerge Primark retail business from Food business, as announced after group review

Outlook

  • Company maintains full-year outlook, except now expects Sugar to post an adjusted operating loss in 2026

  • Group expects 2026 adjusted operating profit and adjusted EPS to be below last year

  • Company says cost impacts from Middle East conflict expected to be manageable in 2026, but risks remain

Result Drivers

  • RETAIL INVESTMENT & MARGIN PRESSURE - Retail profit declined due to higher markdowns, increased investment in product, marketing and digital, and weak like-for-like sales in Europe

  • GROCERY PROFIT DROP - Grocery adjusted operating profit fell 20%, mainly due to weak demand in US oils and higher cocoa costs and tariffs impacting international brands

  • SUGAR LOSS - Sugar segment posted an operating loss, mainly due to lower average selling prices in Europe and higher production costs

Company press release:

data.title: Finland's Ponsse Q1 profit drops due to weak order books
data.description:
Overview

  • Finland forest machine maker's Q1 net sales fell 10% yr/yr amid weak order books

  • Q1 operating profit and EPS dropped sharply from prior year

  • Company maintained 2026 profit guidance, expects operating profit in line with 2025

Outlook

  • Ponsse expects 2026 operating profit to be on par with 2025 (EUR 41.6 mln)

  • Company says economic uncertainty and trade tensions are weighing on demand for forest machines

  • Ponsse to complete obligations under Brazil Full Service agreement during Q2 2026

Result Drivers

  • LOW CAPACITY UTILISATION - Factory operated below capacity for first nine weeks due to weak order books, impacting invoicing and net sales

  • REGIONAL SALES VARIATION - New machine sales developed favourably in Sweden, Central Europe, and Latin America, while Finnish market remained challenging

  • WEAK USED MACHINE SALES - Challenging market conditions continued to affect used machine sales, limiting inventory reduction

Company press release:

  • The average consensus recommendation for the heavy machinery & vehicles peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Ponsse Oyj is €24.00, about 2.6% above its April 20 closing price of €23.40

  • The stock recently traded at 19 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 19 three months ago
